title: Setup Python Environment and Dependencies
description: Install required packages for ProgramClean experiments including vLLM for local LLM inference, data processing libraries, and baseline implementations

steps.1.1_create_venv: Create virtual environment: python -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate
steps.1.2_install_core: Install core dependencies: pip install pandas numpy scipy scikit-learn tqdm
steps.1.3_install_llm: Install vLLM for CPU inference: pip install vllm --extra-index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cpu
steps.1.4_install_analysis: Install analysis/plotting: pip install matplotlib seaborn tabulate
steps.1.5_verify: Verify installations: python -c 'import pandas; import vllm; print("OK")'
steps.1.6_model_download: Download models: huggingface-cli download meta-llama/Llama-3.2-3B-Instruct --local-dir ./models/llama-3.2-3b; huggingface-cli download codellama/CodeLlama-7b-Instruct-hf --local-dir ./models/codellama-7b

title: Download and Prepare Benchmark Datasets
description: Download standard data cleaning benchmarks (Hospital, Flights, Beers) and create novel domain test data. Prepare ground truth labels for evaluation.

steps.2.2_download_hospital: Download Hospital dataset from https://github.com/BigDaMa/raha/tree/master/raha/datasets/hospital. Save to data/hospital/raw/
steps.2.3_download_flights: Download Flights dataset from https://github.com/BigDaMa/raha/tree/master/raha/datasets/flights. Save to data/flights/raw/
steps.2.4_download_beers: Download Beers dataset from https://github.com/BigDaMa/raha/tree/master/raha/datasets/beers. Save to data/beers/raw/
steps.2.5_create_novel: Create novel domain test datasets with 6 semantic types: BTC addresses (100 samples), ETH addresses (100), JWT tokens (100), modern TLD emails (100), UUIDs (100), GitHub repo URLs (100). Include ~20% errors per type.
steps.2.6_ground_truth: Parse ground truth files for Hospital, Flights, Beers datasets. Create CSV with columns: row_id, column_name, original_value, clean_value, is_error.
steps.2.7_statistics: Compute dataset statistics: row counts, column counts, error rates per column, data types. Save to data/{dataset}/stats.json
steps.2.8_split: For each dataset, ensure dirty/clean pairs are aligned. No train/test split needed (unsupervised setting).

title: Implement Data Profiling Utilities
description: Create utilities for extracting column statistics and samples for LLM prompting. This supports Phase 1 (Semantic Profiling) of ProgramClean.

steps.3.1_profiler_class: Implement ColumnProfiler class in src/profiler.py with methods: profile_column(df, col_name) returning {min, max, distinct_count, null_count, pattern_distribution, sample_values}
steps.3.2_pattern_detection: Implement pattern detection: detect_regex_patterns(values) to identify common patterns like dates, phone numbers, IDs
steps.3.3_sample_selection: Implement diverse sampling: select_diverse_samples(values, n=10) that selects representative values covering pattern diversity
steps.3.4_test_profiler: Test profiler on Hospital dataset: python -m pytest tests/test_profiler.py -v

title: Implement ProgramClean Core Components
description: Implement the three-phase ProgramClean pipeline: Semantic Profiling (LLM), Constraint Program Synthesis (LLM), and Program Execution (native Python).

steps.4.1_llm_backend: Implement LLM backend in src/llm_backend.py: class vLLMBackend with generate(prompt, max_tokens=1024, temperature=0.1) method. Support both Llama-3.2-3B and CodeLlama-7b.
steps.4.2_semantic_profiler: Implement semantic profiling in src/semantic_profiler.py: generate_semantic_profile(column_stats, column_name, samples) → structured profile with type, constraints, common_errors
steps.4.3_program_synthesis: Implement program synthesis in src/program_synthesis.py: synthesize_constraint_program(semantic_profile) → Python code string with validate_X(), repair_X(), detect_and_repair() functions
steps.4.4_sandbox: Implement sandboxed execution in src/sandbox.py: execute_program_safely(code, data, timeout=30, max_memory_mb=512) with AST-based validation, restricted builtins, process isolation
steps.4.5_programclean_class: Implement main ProgramClean class in src/programclean.py with methods: fit(df) [profiles columns and synthesizes programs], transform(df) [executes programs], fit_transform(df)
steps.4.6_test_components: Unit test each component: python -m pytest tests/ -v --tb=short

title: Implement Simple Baseline: Raha Statistical Method
description: Implement or adapt Raha as a simple statistical baseline that is fast but semantically blind. This establishes the performance floor for accuracy and ceiling for speed.

steps.5.2_wrapper: Implement wrapper in baselines/raha_baseline.py: RahaBaseline class with fit(dirty_df) and predict() methods returning error detection results
steps.5.3_configure: Configure Raha for fair comparison: use default parameters (configuration-free as per paper), limit to 3 detection strategies for speed
steps.5.4_verify: Test on Hospital dataset: python baselines/raha_baseline.py --dataset hospital --output results/raha/hospital.json

title: Implement Strong Baseline: IterClean-style Direct LLM
description: Implement a simplified version of IterClean that uses LLM for direct cell validation. This is our accuracy benchmark (should be strong but slow). Limited to subset due to cost.

steps.6.1_direct_validation: Implement DirectValidation baseline in baselines/direct_validation.py. For each cell: prompt LLM 'Is value X valid for column Y with semantic type Z? Answer YES/NO'
steps.6.2_batch_prompting: Implement batch prompting for efficiency: validate 10 cells per prompt to reduce API calls
steps.6.3_subset: Limit to 100 cells per dataset for feasibility: sample cells stratified by column
steps.6.4_cache: Implement response caching to avoid re-generating for same values: use sqlite3 cache
steps.6.5_test: Test on Hospital subset: python baselines/direct_validation.py --dataset hospital --max-cells 100 --output results/direct_val/hospital.json

title: Implement SEED-style Compiler Baseline
description: Implement a simplified SEED-style baseline that uses LLM-as-compiler but without unified programs or coupled detection-repair. This isolates the value of our specific compilation approach.

steps.7.1_seed_baseline: Implement SEEDBaseline in baselines/seed_baseline.py: Uses LLM to generate individual validation functions per column (like SEED's CodeGen module) without semantic profiling step
steps.7.2_no_repair: SEED baseline generates detection-only functions (no repair synthesis) to show value of coupled detection-repair
steps.7.3_no_profile: Direct code generation from samples without structured semantic profiling step
steps.7.4_test: Test on all datasets: python baselines/seed_baseline.py --dataset all --output results/seed/

title: Run ProgramClean on All Datasets
description: Execute ProgramClean on all benchmark datasets with both LLM models (Llama-3.2-3B and CodeLlama-7b) to test accuracy and efficiency. Run with 3 random seeds for robustness.

steps.8.1_hospital: Run on Hospital dataset: for seed in 42 43 44; do python experiments/run_programclean.py --dataset hospital --model llama-3.2-3b --seed $seed --output results/programclean/hospital_llama_seed${seed}.json; done
steps.8.2_flights: Run on Flights dataset: for seed in 42 43 44; do python experiments/run_programclean.py --dataset flights --model llama-3.2-3b --seed $seed --output results/programclean/flights_llama_seed${seed}.json; done
steps.8.3_beers: Run on Beers dataset: for seed in 42 43 44; do python experiments/run_programclean.py --dataset beers --model llama-3.2-3b --seed $seed --output results/programclean/beers_llama_seed${seed}.json; done
steps.8.4_novel: Run on novel domain tests: python experiments/run_programclean.py --dataset novel --model llama-3.2-3b --seed 42 --output results/programclean/novel_llama.json
steps.8.5_codellama: Run subset with CodeLlama-7b for comparison: python experiments/run_programclean.py --dataset hospital --model codellama-7b --seed 42 --output results/programclean/hospital_codellama.json
steps.8.6_collect_metrics: Collect all metrics: precision, recall, F1, runtime, llm_calls, program_validity_rate. Save aggregated results.

title: Run Baselines on All Datasets
description: Execute all baselines on the same datasets for fair comparison. Run with same random seeds where applicable.

steps.9.1_raha_all: Run Raha on all datasets: for dataset in hospital flights beers; do python baselines/raha_baseline.py --dataset $dataset --seed 42 --output results/raha/${dataset}.json; done
steps.9.2_direct_val: Run Direct Validation on 100-cell subsets: for dataset in hospital flights beers; do python baselines/direct_validation.py --dataset $dataset --max-cells 100 --seed 42 --output results/direct_val/${dataset}.json; done
steps.9.3_seed_baseline: Run SEED baseline on all datasets: for dataset in hospital flights beers novel; do python baselines/seed_baseline.py --dataset $dataset --seed 42 --output results/seed/${dataset}.json; done

title: Ablation Study: Semantic Profiling vs Direct Code Gen
description: Test the value of the semantic profiling step by comparing ProgramClean (full) against a variant that skips profiling and generates code directly from samples.

steps.10.1_naive_variant: Implement NaiveCodeGen variant in ablations/naive_codegen.py: Directly prompts LLM to generate constraint code from samples without structured semantic profile
steps.10.2_run_ablation: Run on Hospital and Beers datasets: for dataset in hospital beers; do python ablations/naive_codegen.py --dataset $dataset --seed 42 --output results/ablations/naive_${dataset}.json; done
steps.10.3_compare: Compare F1 scores: ProgramClean full vs Naive CodeGen. Expectation: ProgramClean has higher accuracy due to structured semantic understanding

title: Ablation Study: Detection-Only vs Detection+Repair
description: Test the value of coupled repair synthesis by comparing ProgramClean (with repair) against a detection-only variant.

steps.11.1_detection_only: Implement DetectionOnly variant in ablations/detection_only.py: Uses ProgramClean but only generates validate functions (no repair synthesis)
steps.11.2_run_ablation: Run on Hospital and Beers datasets: for dataset in hospital beers; do python ablations/detection_only.py --dataset $dataset --seed 42 --output results/ablations/detonly_${dataset}.json; done
steps.11.3_compare: Compare detection F1 and repair accuracy. Expectation: Detection F1 similar; repair capability only in full version

title: Ablation Study: Model Size Comparison
description: Compare performance across different model sizes to understand the compute-accuracy tradeoff.

steps.12.1_download_small: Download smaller model for comparison: huggingface-cli download TinyLlama/TinyLlama-1.1B-Chat-v1.0 --local-dir ./models/tinyllama-1b
steps.12.2_run_small: Run ProgramClean with TinyLlama-1B on Hospital dataset: python experiments/run_programclean.py --dataset hospital --model tinyllama-1b --seed 42 --output results/ablations/hospital_tinyllama.json
steps.12.3_compare_models: Compare F1 across: TinyLlama-1B, Llama-3.2-3B, CodeLlama-7B. Analyze if larger models improve synthesis quality

title: Program Validity and Semantic Correctness Analysis
description: Measure the rate of syntactically valid programs, execution errors, and semantic bugs (correct syntax but wrong logic).

steps.13.1_validity_rate: Analyze all synthesized programs: compute percentage that execute without errors. Target: >90%
steps.13.2_error_categories: Categorize execution errors: syntax errors, import errors, runtime exceptions, timeout errors
steps.13.3_semantic_audit: Manual audit of 30 randomly selected programs for semantic correctness. Check: does validate function correctly encode column semantics?
steps.13.4_self_validation: Test self-validation mechanism: does synthetic violation detection catch semantic bugs? Measure precision of self-validation

title: Security and Sandboxing Validation
description: Validate that the sandboxing effectively prevents malicious code execution while allowing legitimate constraint programs.

steps.14.1_test_sandbox: Create test cases with malicious patterns: file I/O, network access, code injection. Verify all are blocked.
steps.14.2_resource_limits: Test resource limits: programs exceeding 30s timeout or 512MB memory are terminated. Verify.
steps.14.3_legitimate: Test that legitimate constraint programs (regex, datetime parsing) execute successfully within sandbox

title: Compute Cost and Runtime Analysis
description: Measure and compare computational costs: runtime, LLM API calls, memory usage across all methods.

steps.15.1_runtime_compare: Compare wall-clock time: ProgramClean vs Raha vs Direct Validation vs SEED baseline. Compute speedup factors.
steps.15.2_llm_calls: Count LLM invocations: ProgramClean (O(columns)) vs Direct Validation (O(cells)). Verify 100x+ reduction.
steps.15.3_memory_profile: Measure peak memory usage for each method using memory_profiler
steps.15.4_cost_estimate: Estimate cloud LLM API cost: multiply token counts by pricing. Show ProgramClean cost advantage

title: Success Criteria Validation
description: Evaluate all results against the predefined success and refutation criteria from the proposal.

steps.16.1_rq1_accuracy: RQ1 (Accuracy): Check if ProgramClean F1 is within 10% of Direct Validation (IterClean proxy). Report: achieved/not achieved
steps.16.2_rq2_speed: RQ2 (Speed): Check if ProgramClean is 100x faster than Direct Validation. Report speedup factor
steps.16.3_rq3_zeroshot: RQ3 (Zero-shot): Check if ProgramClean succeeds on novel domain types (BTC, JWT, etc.) that Auto-Test cannot handle
steps.16.4_rq4_ablation: RQ4 (Ablation): Check if ProgramClean matches Direct Validation accuracy. Validate compilation value
steps.16.5_confirmation_check: Confirmation criteria: Check all 5 criteria from proposal. Count how many are met
steps.16.6_refutation_check: Refutation criteria: Check if any refutation condition is triggered. If so, document findings

title: Generate Result Figures and Tables
description: Create publication-ready figures and tables for the paper. Include error bars for multi-seed experiments.

steps.17.1_fig_accuracy: Create Figure 1: Bar chart comparing F1 scores across methods (ProgramClean, Raha, Direct Validation, SEED baseline) with error bars from 3 seeds
steps.17.2_fig_runtime: Create Figure 2: Log-scale runtime comparison showing speedup factors
steps.17.3_fig_ablation: Create Figure 3: Ablation study results showing contribution of each component
steps.17.4_table_results: Create Table 1: Comprehensive results table with Precision, Recall, F1, Runtime, LLM calls for each method and dataset
steps.17.5_table_novel: Create Table 2: Novel domain test results showing zero-shot generalization
steps.17.6_fig_program_validity: Create Figure 4: Pie chart showing program validity rates and error categories
steps.17.7_save_outputs: Save all figures to figures/ directory in PDF and PNG formats. Save tables as LaTeX and CSV.

title: Generate Final Result Summary
description: Create comprehensive summary of all experimental results with statistical significance testing.

steps.18.1_aggregate: Aggregate all results across seeds: compute mean and std for each metric
steps.18.2_significance: Perform statistical significance tests (paired t-test) comparing ProgramClean to baselines
steps.18.3_summary_json: Generate results/summary.json with all aggregated metrics and significance results
steps.18.4_summary_report: Generate results/SUMMARY.md with narrative description of findings, answering each RQ
